mappings.rationaleAI: The Earth's core covering seismological evidence for solid inner and liquid outer core, iron/nickel composition, and dynamo theory comprehensively addresses the Earth's concentric layers (A), the mantle (A.2.c), the core (A.2.d), and geophysical evidence (D.1).

Seismology revealing crustal, mantle, and core divisions through seismic wave analysis, earthquake causes, distribution, and magnitude scales comprehensively addresses the basic divisions (A.2), earthquakes (B), and geophysical evidence (D.1).

Plate tectonics covering crust, mantle convection, subduction, earthquake distribution, and the Mohorovicic discontinuity addresses the basic divisions of the solid Earth (A.2), earthquake causes (B.1), and distribution (B.2).
Earth's formation from the nebular hypothesis, differentiation into core/mantle/crust, and the origin of atmosphere and oceans addresses the development of Earth's structure and composition (A.3).
Radiometric dating using radioactive decay and isotope geochemistry to determine Earth's age at 4.54 billion years addresses geochemical evidence for inferring Earth's interior composition (D.2).
Catastrophism and the asteroid impact hypothesis for K-Pg extinction, along with geological forces shaping Earth, provides context for earthquake causes and sudden geological events (B.1).
